text: <par>This Chantyal form is a borrowing from Nepali <latinform>anuhār</latinform>, <latinform>anwār</latinform> 'likeness, appearance, face'. See Turner (1966:16) #342.</par>
<par>Second syllable <latinform>bɦəri</latinform> is a borrowing from Nepali 'full'.</par>
<par>The last two syllables are probably a borrowing from Nepali <latinform>pèmdash ato</latinform> ‘shoulder’ , but if not they could be tagged 570 *ba SHOULDER + 3349 *ta SHOULDER.</par>
<par>Compare the tagging of this form and #127722.</par>
<par>As Sunwar above; also below to page end.M2: 292? or 66? (THROUGHOUT)</par>
<par>See below next section; also Limbu p. 4.</par>
<par>division? 1385??? (or does this belong with prev. record?)</par>
<par>Last syllable is from Nepali “house” . Are first two syllables “child” plus genetive particle?</par>
